levels.name: critical
levels.definition: A credible path to conflicting finality, unauthorized state or supply change, production key compromise, signature/proof forgery, systemic loss of consensus-required data, or an operational failure that can irreversibly compromise the network.

levels.name: high
levels.definition: A material safety, liveness, confidentiality, economic, or operational failure requiring substantial capability, limited scope, or recoverable impact, without an established S1 path.

levels.name: moderate
levels.definition: A bounded weakness, hardening gap, or defense-in-depth failure with limited direct impact under the declared threat model.

levels.name: low
levels.definition: A minor weakness, documentation defect, or maintainability concern without a demonstrated material security impact.
